message:
config: bump default example to Claude Opus 4.7

4.7 is the latest Opus model (1M context, improved capability
over 4.6). Update the example env so new installations use the
current flagship by default. Existing installations must update
their own /etc/supervised-agent/<instance>.env files separately —
install.sh only seeds the example.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -22,7 +22,7 @@ AGENT_WORKDIR=/home/dev/my-project
 # For Claude Code, do NOT pass -p; it exits after one reply.
 # IMPORTANT: quote multi-word values. install.sh sources this file with
 # `set -a; . "$ENV_FILE"` so any unquoted flags get parsed as commands.
-AGENT_LAUNCH_CMD="/usr/bin/claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model claude-opus-4-6"
+AGENT_LAUNCH_CMD="/usr/bin/claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model claude-opus-4-7"
 
 # The text sent to the agent after its TUI is ready. For Claude Code this is
 # almost always a /loop command. Keep it short and point the agent at a policy
